files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentExecutionMode
files.Summary: Strategy the AgentOrchestrator uses to schedule a set of agents.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentMessage
files.Summary: An immutable, timestamped log entry produced by an agent and appended to the shared blackboard.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentMessageLevel
files.Summary: Severity of an recorded on a .

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentOrchestrationOptions
files.Summary: Options that control how the schedules and bounds an agentic run.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentOrchestrationResult
files.Summary: The immutable outcome of an run. It exposes point-in-time snapshots only; the live, mutable is intentionally not surfaced so callers cannot retroactively change the projected result.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentOrchestrator
files.Summary: Coordinates the assessment agents over a single , scheduling them according to their declared and the requested , isolating per-agent failures, and finishing with the validation pass.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentResult
files.Summary: Immutable record of how a single agent run finished. Recorded on the so the orchestrator can isolate failures and the ValidatorAgent can reason about completeness.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentRole
files.Summary: Identifies the assessment domain an owns and the kind of output it contributes to a .

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AgentRunStatus
files.Summary: Terminal outcome of a single run.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.AssessmentAgentBase
files.Summary: Base class for assessment agents that centralises run timing, structured logging, and the failure-isolation contract so concrete agents only implement their domain logic.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.CosmosAnalyzerAgent
files.Summary: Agent that owns the Cosmos DB analysis domain. It wraps the existing (whose signatures are intentionally unchanged) and commits the resulting to the shared context for downstream agents.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.DataQualityAgent
files.Summary: Agent that owns the pre-migration data-quality domain. It wraps the existing (unchanged) and commits the resulting to the shared context.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.IAssessmentAgent
files.Summary: A single-responsibility participant in an agentic assessment run. Each agent owns one , reads any upstream outputs it needs from the shared context, performs its work, and commits its own output back to the context.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.ISharedAssessmentContext
files.Summary: Thread-safe shared blackboard through which assessment agents communicate. Agents read the outputs produced by upstream agents, contribute their own domain output exactly once, append log messages, and record their run results. Implemented by .

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.SharedAssessmentContext
files.Summary: Thread-safe blackboard implementation of . A single private lock guards every piece of mutable state (the four domain outputs, the message log, and the result log) so concurrently scheduled agents observe a consistent view.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.SqlPlannerAgent
files.Summary: Agent that owns the Azure SQL migration-planning domain. It wraps the existing (unchanged) and commits the resulting to the shared context.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.ValidationFinding
files.Summary: A single observation produced by the while cross-checking agent outputs.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.ValidationFindingCategory
files.Summary: Classifies a by the kind of problem it represents, so consumers can reason about which findings affect run completeness versus cross-output consistency versus pure diagnostics.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.ValidationReport
files.Summary: The typed verdict produced by the after cross-checking the outputs of the other agents. It is the stable, structured surface downstream consumers (and the orchestrator/CLI) use to decide whether an agentic run succeeded.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.ValidatorAgent
files.Summary: Agent that cross-checks the outputs produced by the other agents and emits a typed describing whether the run is complete and internally consistent.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.DataFactoryArtifact<TProps>
files.Summary: Generic ADF resource envelope: { "name": ..., "properties": { "type": ..., ... } }. The envelope is strongly typed; on the concrete Properties bag keeps everything extensible so later sub-issues (#142–#147) can layer parameters, retry policies, monitoring, etc. without breaking #141's shape.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.DatasetProperties

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.DatasetResource

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.LinkedServiceResource

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.PipelineActivity
files.Summary: Pipeline activity. Activity type (e.g. Copy, ExecutePipeline) is the discriminator; activity-specific configuration lives under . Retry policy / on-failure / depends-on / linked-service refs are layered in by follow-on sub-issues via the extension bag.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.PipelineProperties

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.PipelineResource

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.PropertiesBase

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory.ResourceReference

ADF uses this shape everywhere (datasets in inputs/outputs, linked services on datasets, pipelines in ExecutePipeline activities, etc.). The optional bag is used by callers that pass values into a parameterised dataset, linked service, or pipeline reference.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Reporting.ReportGenerationService
files.Summary: Service responsible for generating comprehensive Excel and Word reports Implements Azure best practices for reporting and documentation

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.CosmosDbAnalysisService
files.Summary: Service for analyzing Cosmos DB databases and collecting performance metrics Implements Azure best practices with proper authentication and error handling

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ActivityPolicyBuilder
files.Summary: Builds the ADF policy block for an activity (#143). Pure / side-effect free — returns a serialiser-ready dictionary which the orchestrator and copy-activity builder stash on .

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.AdfJsonSerializer
files.Summary: Single source of truth for ADF JSON serialization options. All artifact writers MUST route through this class so output is deterministic, camelCase, indented, and skips nulls — the shape Azure Data Factory imports/deploys cleanly.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.AdfNameRegistry
files.Summary: Allocates collision-proof, ADF-compliant identifiers for activities, datasets, linked services, pipelines, and the on-disk file names that mirror them. ADF allows up to 260 chars for most resources but activity names are capped at 55. We use the tighter budget for everything so a single sanitizer covers all artifacts. Case-insensitive collisions get a stable 8-char hash suffix derived from the full source identity.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ArmParameterDefinition
files.Summary: ARM-template parameter declaration. ARM uses { "type": ..., "defaultValue": ..., "metadata": { "description": ... } } shape — distinct from the deployment-parameter file's { "value": ..., "metadata": ... } shape (which lives in adf-parameters.template.json).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ArmResourceInput
files.Summary: One ADF artifact slated for inclusion in the ARM template.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ArmTemplateBuilder
files.Summary:
Builds the deployable Azure Resource Manager template that wraps every emitted ADF artifact (linked services, datasets, pipelines) under Microsoft.DataFactory/factories/{kind} resources (#146).

Design notes:

  • Serialise-then-parse input: every per-artifact properties bag is captured as a (via JsonSerializer.SerializeToNode) so [JsonExtensionData] AdditionalProperties is already flattened — the ARM template payload matches what's written to the per-artifact JSON file byte-for-byte (modulo the targeted defaultValue rewrite below).
  • Generic reference walker: recurses through every / in the properties bag looking for { "type": "*Reference", "referenceName": <name> } triples. This automatically catches Dataset / LinkedService / Pipeline references plus the Key Vault LinkedServiceReference nested under typeProperties.accountKey.store on the Cosmos/SQL linked services — no per-activity-type code needed.
  • Closed-world dependsOn: edges are only emitted for resources actually present in this template's resource set. External / operator-managed linked services (e.g. a fault-tolerance log storage LS the operator has pre-provisioned) are silently skipped here — the operator's existing deployment of those resources satisfies them at New-AzResourceGroupDeployment time.
  • Targeted defaultValue rewrite: when a pipeline's map is non-null, the builder walks properties.parameters.<pipelineParamName>.defaultValue and replaces the literal with [parameters('<armParamName>')]. This is the only mechanism that actually flows deployment-time parameter values into deployed ADF pipelines — without it, the parameter file would be decorative.
  • Determinism: resources sorted by (kind, name) and dependsOn entries sorted alphabetically so the file diff is stable across runs.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.CopyActivityBuilder.BuildResult
files.Summary: Pairs the Copy activity produced by with any warnings surfaced during translator-mapping construction (e.g. skipped transformed fields, deferred child tables).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.CopyActivityBuilder
files.Summary: Translates a into a fully-formed ADF Copy activity. Field mappings flagged RequiresTransformation are deliberately skipped here (annotated on the activity + surfaced as a warning) so #145 (data validation / mapping data flows) can take over without rewriting this stage.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.CopyActivityPolicy
files.Summary: ADF policy shape applied to every emitted Copy activity (#143).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.DataFactoryGenerationOptions
files.Summary: Behaviour toggles for the ADF generator. Defaults match the foundational #141 scope extended by #142 (parameterised, environment-agnostic artifacts); follow-on sub-issues add new properties (never replace existing).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.DataFactoryGenerationResult
files.Summary: Result returned by . Surfaces the list of files written and any warnings the operator should review (e.g. transformed fields skipped, child tables deferred, pipeline chunking applied).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.DataFactoryPipelineGenerationService
files.Summary:
Orchestrates Azure Data Factory artifact generation: composes the per-database linked service, all source / sink datasets, one Copy activity per , and writes every artifact under <outputDirectory>/ADF/{LinkedServices,Datasets,Pipelines}/.

Pipelines are split per source database, and any database with more than mappings is chunked into multiple pipeline files so ADF's per-pipeline activity limit is respected. A master orchestrator pipeline references every per-database pipeline via ExecutePipeline activities and forwards per-database parameters down.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.DatasetBuilder
files.Summary: Builds ADF source / sink datasets for every Cosmos container and every target SQL table. Datasets declare parameters (e.g. databaseName, collectionName) and forward them into the linked-service reference and typeProperties so the same dataset can be reused across environments by passing parameter values from the invoking pipeline (#142).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.DiagnosticSettingsTemplateBuilder
files.Summary: Emits a stand-alone, deployable ARM template containing a Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings resource scoped to the target Data Factory. Sets logAnalyticsDestinationType = "Dedicated" so logs land in the resource-specific ADFPipelineRun / ADFActivityRun tables (matching the bundled KQL queries) rather than the legacy AzureDiagnostics table.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ExecutePipelinePolicy
files.Summary: ADF policy shape applied to every emitted ExecutePipeline activity (#143).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.FailureNotificationBuilder.NotificationPair
files.Summary: Pairs the Web (webhook POST) and Fail (re-throw) activities emitted by for a single upstream activity failure.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.FailureNotificationBuilder
files.Summary: Builds the Web + Fail activity pair that fires when an upstream activity fails (#143). The Web activity POSTs to a parameterised webhook (Logic App / Teams / PagerDuty / etc) with the failed activity's error message, and a subsequent Fail activity re-throws so the pipeline still reports failure to its caller. The Web activity has policy.secureInput/secureOutput set so the webhook URL never enters run history.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.FaultToleranceOptions
files.Summary: Copy-activity fault tolerance options (#143).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator
files.Summary: Generates ready-to-deploy Azure Data Factory artifacts (linked services, datasets, pipelines) from an . Foundational sub-issue #141 of parent #70: copy activities per container → table mapping. Later sub-issues extend the output with parameters, retry policy, monitoring, validation, ARM templating, and incremental-load support.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.IncrementalCopyActivityBuilder
files.Summary:
Builds the per-mapping incremental activity group (#147). For each container → table mapping selected for incremental copy, the orchestrator inserts:

  1. LookupWatermark_<m> — Azure SQL Lookup with a literal-mapping-key SELECT against the watermark table.
  2. SetVariable_LastTs_<m> — Reads the Lookup output into lastWatermark_<m>.
  3. SetVariable_NewTs_<m> — Computes newWatermark_<m> = max(lastWatermark, unix(utcnow()) - safetyLag). The clamp prevents safety-lag arithmetic from producing a backwards window.
  4. (when validation on) LookupSrc_<c> with incremental WHERE.
  5. The Copy activity (passed in pre-built; this builder rewrites typeProperties.source.query).
  6. ScriptUpdateWatermark_<m> — Script (NonQuery) running the MERGE with backwards-protection guard, depends on Copy Succeeded.

Mapping key format: <srcDb>::<srcContainer>->[<tgtDb>].[<schema>].[<table>]. This composite key prevents same-named containers across different source databases (or fan-out to multiple sink tables) from corrupting each other's watermark — see rubber-duck Blocker B5.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.IncrementalCopyMode
files.Summary: Incremental copy implementation strategy (#147). Only is implemented in this sub-issue. The ChangeFeedDataFlow slot is reserved for parent #69 to add a Mapping-Data-Flow-based change-feed implementation alongside, without breaking the public option surface.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.IncrementalCopyOptions
files.Summary:
Incremental copy configuration (#147). Default is false so output is byte-identical to the post-#146 baseline unless the operator opts in.

When enabled, each per-mapping Copy is wrapped in a LookupWatermark → SetVariable_LastTs → SetVariable_NewTs → [LookupSrc] → Copy → ScriptUpdateWatermark chain. The Copy's typeProperties.source.query is overridden with a WHERE c._ts > lastTs AND c._ts <= newTs predicate. The newTs is clamped to utcnow() - WatermarkSafetyLagSeconds to avoid losing documents committed within the boundary second (Cosmos _ts is second-granular).

Mapping Data Flow change-feed support (the second Microsoft-documented incremental pattern, exposed as enableChangeFeed / startFromBeginning on the data-flow source) is intentionally NOT in this sub-issue. The IncrementalCopyMode.ChangeFeedDataFlow enum slot is reserved for parent #69 to add later without breaking the option surface.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.IncrementalGroup
files.Summary: Per-mapping activity group produced by . Names + variables surface to the orchestrator so the per-db pipeline can declare matching parameters, variables, and (optionally) a parameter-template entry.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.JsonPathEscaper
files.Summary: Builds JSONPath expressions for ADF translator mappings. Bracket-notation is used for every segment so field names containing dots, hyphens, spaces, or quotes work without manual escaping by the operator.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.LinkedServiceBuilder
files.Summary: Builds the parameterised ADF linked services. Default emit mode is System-Assigned Managed Identity for both connectors (the modern recommended shape). Key Vault wiring is opt-in via .

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.MonitoringOptions
files.Summary: Monitoring & logging configuration (#144). Defaults are deployment-safe: the diagnostic-settings ARM template is emitted, every Copy activity gets monitoring-friendly userProperties, and the KQL cheat-sheet is written out so operators can drop the queries into Log Analytics on day one.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ParameterCatalog.ParameterDefinition
files.Summary: Typed descriptor for a single ADF parameter: the Name surfaced in generated artifact JSON, the ADF Type token (e.g. "string"), a human-readable Description, and an optional DefaultValue baked into the parameter block at generation time.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ParameterCatalog
files.Summary: Single source of truth for every ADF parameter name used by the generated artifacts. Centralising the names means linked-service builders, dataset builders, copy-activity builders, the orchestrator's adf-parameters.template.json, and the unit tests all reference identical strings — typos cause test failures, not silent runtime errors.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.SinkWriteBehavior
files.Summary: ADF Copy-activity sink write behaviour controlling whether rows are inserted fresh or upserted using target-table key columns.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.SqlIdentifierEscaper
files.Summary: Bracket-quotes T-SQL identifiers using SQL Server rules: [name], with any literal ] doubled to ]]. Centralised so #145 row-count validation queries (and any future generated T-SQL) cannot be tricked by an assessment-supplied identifier that contains ].

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.SqlLiteralEscaper
files.Summary: Escapes T-SQL string literals by doubling single quotes (' → ''). Used by #147 incremental queries that bake the mapping key as a literal into a sqlReaderQuery / Script body (the alternative would be ADF Lookup parameter binding, which Azure SQL Lookup does not support without switching to SqlServerStoredProcedure).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.UserPropertiesBuilder
files.Summary: Builds the ADF activity-level userProperties list that flows into the UserProperties custom-dimension column on the ADFActivityRun Log Analytics table (when the diagnostic setting uses logAnalyticsDestinationType = "Dedicated"). Centralising the shape means every Copy activity in every pipeline emits the same monitoring contract.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ValidationActivityBuilder.ValidationTriplet
files.Summary: Triplet returned by . Caller adds these to the pipeline activity list and wires the Copy activity's dependsOn to . Total activity contribution (including nested Fail inside IfCondition) is 4.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ValidationActivityBuilder
files.Summary:
Builds the (LookupSource, LookupTarget, IfCondition) activity triplet that performs row-count parity validation around a Copy activity (#145).

Pattern (per Microsoft Learn): LookupSrc → Copy → LookupTgt → IfCondition(expression) └ ifFalseActivities: [Fail]

Caller is responsible for chaining the Copy activity's dependsOn to the LookupSource so it doesn't start before the baseline count is captured.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ValidationOptions
files.Summary: Row-count validation configuration (#145). Defaults are conservative: validation is enabled with strict equality and zero tolerance. Operators that expect drift (e.g. mid-load Cosmos writes, soft-deleted rows) can opt down to with a tolerance budget.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.ValidationStrategy
files.Summary: Row-count validation comparison strategy (#145).

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory.WatermarkSchemaBuilder
files.Summary:
Generates the idempotent T-SQL DDL for the per-mapping watermark table that the #147 incremental copy pattern reads from / writes to. The same DDL is emitted as a stand-alone .sql file under ADF/SQL/ and (when is on) embedded inside an ADF Script activity at the start of every per-db pipeline so the pipeline self-bootstraps the table on first run.

Schema: [mappingKey] NVARCHAR(450) PRIMARY KEY — composite key chosen to fit SQL Server's 900-byte index limit (NVARCHAR(450) = 900 bytes). [lastTs] BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — Unix seconds; matches Cosmos _ts domain. [updatedUtc] DATETIME2(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT SYSUTCDATETIME() — audit column; not used by the runtime but useful for ops.

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.ValidationScriptGeneratorService

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.SqlProject.DeploymentOptions

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.SqlProject.SqlDatabaseProject

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.SqlProject.SqlDatabaseProjectService

files.Title: CosmosToSqlAssessment.SqlProject.SqlProjectMetadata
files.Summary: Additional metadata for SQL Database Project
